Chemical concentrate for Chemical Foam operations: Four cabin installed 
storage tanks with total maximum chemical load of 710 kg (1,566 lb). Volume 
600 litres (132 Imp., 158 US gallons). 
Additional Cargo Limitations 
(a) Carriage of general cargo in the cabin is prohibited when the water or 
chemical concentrate or 
chemical/water 
mix tanks are in use. 
(b) Landing with jettisonable water or chemical/water load in the tanks is 
prohibited. 
(c) All chemicals to be used in Liquid Spraying Operations must be 
approved by the local environmental authorities prior to commencement 
of spraying. 
(d) All chemicals to be used in chemical Foam operations must comply with .-, 
Canadair Material Specification CMS.560-01 and be approved by the local 
environmental authorities prior to commencement of foam fire fighting 
operations.

Longitudinal: Lugs on left hand nose wheel well sidewall at stations 170.0 
and 182.5. Lateral: Lugs on front face of nose wheel well rear bulkhead, 
station 222.50.

NOTE 1 Definitions: For the purposes of this Type Certificate and with reference to 
information on aircraft weight and C.G., the following definitions apply: 
(a) Restricted Category - - Aircraft configured such that water or 
Chemical/ Water Mix may be loaded and promptly jettisoned. 
(b) Utility 
Cateporv - - Aircraft other than those which come within the 
definition of Restricted Category. 
CL-215-1 
A10 
Canadair 
Service Bulletin 215-052 provides instructions for conversion 
between restricted and utility category configurations.

NOTE 2 Carriage of Persons 
(a) The carriage of persons in the cabin of Utility Category Aircraft is not 
permitted unless the modifications defined in the following Canadair 
Service Bulletins are embodied:

(b) The carriage of more than eight persons, but limited to a maximum 
of nineteen persons*, with the exception of item (iv) below, in the 
cabin of Utility Category Aircraft is not permitted unless: 
(i) An approved seating arrangement is incorporated. 
(ii) In addition to the modifications defined in (a) above, the 
modification defined in Canadair Service Bulletin number 
CL215-124 
is embodied 
(SIN 
1001 
to 1030 only). This 
Service Bulletin is not applicable to the 
CL-215-6Bll 
model. 
(iii) A seating arrangement of fourteen persons is approved for 
aircraft 
S/N 
1058 and 1059 in accordance with Modification 
Summary 30065, except as specified in NOTE 4. 
*(iv) 
A seating arrangement of twenty six persons is approved 
for aircraft 
S/N 
1062 and 1063 in accordance with 
Modification Summaries 30070 and 30071, except as 
specified in NOTE 4.

NOTE 2 (c) The carriage of persons in the cabin of Restricted Category Aircraft is 
(Cont'd) only permitted when: 
i. Such persons are Cargo Handlers or persons employed in 
support of the operation; and 
ii. The water tanks are not in use. 
(d) The S.A.R. Configuration permits the carriage of a Flight 
Engmeer 
in the 
cockpit, and Navigator, two observers, plus four more persons in the 
cabin. 
(S/N 
1031 and subsequent only). 
NOTE 3 
NOTE 4 
NOTE 5 
NOTG 6 
All required Placards must be installed in the specified locations. 
Aircraft S/N 1058 and 1059 are not eligible for Canadian Certificate of 
Airworthiness until compliance has been shown with requirements FAR 
25.813(a) 
and 25.815. (Reference DOT Telex LIAE 120 dated 17 June 1978). 
Aircraft S/N 1062 and 1063 are not eligible for Canadian Certificate of 
Airworthiness until compliance has been shown with requirements FAR 
25.803,25.809,25.813(a) 
and 
25.875(b). 
(Reference DOT Telex LIAE 10 dated 
22 January 1979). 
The aircraft must be operated in accordance with all sections of the 
Approved Flight Manual as listed in the Approved Publications. 
Passenger Operation 
As provided for in the Basis of Certification, aircraft registered as 
Commercial Aircraft under Part 
I1 
of the Air Regulations are eligible for such 
domestic passenger operations as may be approved by the Department of 
Transport. 
NOTE 7 The current Weight and Balance Report, containing the list of equipment 
included in the approved empty weight and loading instructions, must be 
provided for each aircraft. 
NOTE 8 The 
CL-215-llA10 
and CL-215-6Bll aircraft do not fully meet the 
airworthiness standards prescribed in Part 
I11 
of Annex 8 of the Convention 
on International Civil Aviation for the carriage of passengers in international 
air navigation.
